summary:
The child recently completed their full rabies vaccination series; thus, no additional vaccines are necessary unless there’s clear high-risk exposure indicating otherwise. Given just an oozing minor injury, routine follow-up rather than immediate intervention aligns best based on available information about post-immunized individuals' response dynamics against potential reexposures within this timeframe.
The next step should be monitoring for signs indicative of more serious infection while ensuring proper hygiene practices at home until further evaluation confirms safety regarding possible rabies
